message:
fix(security): harden AuthKey seed derivation and AuthenticationTag kind-mixing

AuthKey::from_seed previously used DetRng with a special-cased zero-seed
remap to avoid xorshift lockup. This was fragile — the magic constant
0x9e37_79b9_7f4a_7c15 could collide with a real user seed, and the
derivation depended on DetRng internals.

Replace with domain-separated SHA-256 expansion:
  SHA-256("asupersync::security::AuthKey::from_seed:v1" || seed_le_bytes)
This is self-contained, collision-resistant, and independent of DetRng's
zero-state normalization behavior.

AuthenticationTag::compute did not mix SymbolKind into the tag, meaning a
Source symbol and a Repair symbol with identical id/sbn/esi/data would
produce the same authentication tag. This violated the expectation that
tags are domain-specific — a source tag should never verify against a
repair symbol and vice versa.

Fix by XOR-mixing a kind-dependent byte (0x53 for Source, 0xA7 for
Repair) into the final tag byte. The test that previously documented
this as a "known Phase 0 limitation" now asserts the invariant holds.

Changes:
- src/security/key.rs: SHA-256 domain-separated seed expansion
- src/security/tag.rs: SymbolKind byte mixed into tag computation
- tests/security/key_tests.rs: zero-seed vs legacy-magic collision test
- tests/security/tag_tests.rs: cross-kind verification rejection test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
